ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu besewɔ gyoo degbele man toŋ boo keŋ gyu diŋgyiŋ. Keŋte balaŋ burum kɔŋawɔ kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ boo nɔɔman botɔɔ.
translated: When they saw Jesus, they were amazed and said, "This is the son of David." They were filled with wonder and said, "He has done all things well."

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ɛ kɛɛ gyebuse, bɔɔ ne duu abɔɔ ya te bɔɔ ne kɔɔ ya te bɔɔ dana kpeese yaa denɛɛ ŋan man baa see weenɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ ne fa wɔ weenɛɛ. Kaboena ya gyeŋ nyi, ɛ wose ne hia kela gyebuse.
translated: If you are looking for something, do not be discouraged.  If you search diligently, you will surely find it.  Do not be afraid to ask for help.  If you are patient, you will succeed.

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: na Anderiase na Filipo na Batolomeo na Mateo na Tomase na Alfeose bu Gyemisi na Tadeose na Kaanan baale Simɔn ŋon ɔ ne gyae ɔ balaŋ nideli nɛ,
translated: And Andreas and Philip and Bartholomew and Matthew and Thomas and Alpheus and Gyemisi and Thaddeus and Simon the Canaanite were all together.

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu kyaŋa siayɛlɛɛsate ŋon nyiŋmaa man te ɔ moo e lee donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ tɔɔ nɔɔloŋ woli ɔ sia te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa gyakaa ɔ dɔɔ te ɔ bɔɔse e nyiaa, “N ne naa kolo?”
translated: But Jesus said to them, "Have you not read that the one who made them from the beginning made them male and female, and said, 'Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and hold fast to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh'?”

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Balee ŋonaŋ kena Yesu ne kɔŋ lemlem nɛ, te ɔ yeuwɔ ke buŋ ɔ siaman.
translated: Balee, I have come to know Jesus and I have found peace, for he has saved me.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Nawolo nyi be popou naa e te gyakoloŋ kyaŋa wɔ. Debokenaŋ man te Yesu kolosiwɔ na wɔ nyi, “Ɛ nyiŋ konɔɔ! Ɛ na yee ya! Maŋ e.”
translated: Then they said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or shall we look for another?”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Lee weese kaseŋ dɛɛ kelii ɔ ke gɛɛse nɛ, ditiŋtɛɛ kaa lɛɛ tɛɛle keŋ dɔɔ pou.
translated: If you want to know something, ask and it will be given to you.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Kenaŋ wɔle te Yesu na ɔ kaseela baŋ toŋa boo keŋ gyu Gerasenetena tɛɛle man.
translated: When Jesus arrived at the region of Gerasenes, he encountered a man who was possessed by demons.

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Mɔ mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, Elia taŋ kekɔŋ te balaŋ yɛɛ e kpene kamasɛ keŋ be gyae ŋgba mena keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see be yako lee ɔ wose man nɛ.” Yesu kekyɔ tobaale ŋon ɔ na wee keleuleuse nɛ Mateo
translated: If you are going to the village, tell him, "Elia said to me, 'I will go and see the king, and I will tell him what you have done.'" Jesus said to them, "Go and tell him, 'Behold, I am sending you to heal the sick and to raise the dead.'" Matthew

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Ɔ kɛɛ ŋmatee ŋa nɛ, ŋaale yalawɔ gbɛɛneŋbia ŋan doo wɔɔ keŋ man nɛ man, te gyebuse kaa tɔɔsee ŋa di.
translated: If you are going to be a thief, be a good thief, do not steal from your own people, for it is not good to steal from your own.

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: Yesu besewɔ gyɔ dudu fa wɔ nyiaa, “Debaŋ kenaŋ man na Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ gyae ke yɛɛ ŋgba balee ŋon ɔ ne gyae waa gyu gbɛɛ, te ɔ be baake ɔ tewulɛɛ te ɔ be kpɛlɛɛ wa abɔɔ do wɔ be nyiŋmaa man nɛ.
translated: Jesus said to them, "If you knew who I am, you would have known who sent me. But I have come into the world for judgment, so that those who do not see may see, and those who see may become blind."

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu faree wɔ nideli nyi, “Ɛ na yele ŋolo na ŋolo ke nyii nombia kɛŋa ya.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Have you not read that He who made them at the beginning ‘made them male and female,’

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Me dana gbɛɛ keŋ nyi maa mo maŋ gbagba me kɔba yɛɛ kpene keŋ mɛɛ gyae. Keŋ me dana wɔe dɔɔ te na wee neŋ nɛ?”
translated: Tell me what you want me to do so that I can do it for you. What do you want me to do?

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Mɔna siakaralatena baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ moo nuŋ do porontiase man tela kpu na ŋan doo be kanease man nɛ.
translated: The moon is shining brightly, it is time to go home, let us go before it gets dark.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Yesu ka seŋ botɔɔ ɔ ne kolosi nɛ, baale leewɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man kegyia Gyariose ŋon dɛɛ kaa yako e nyiaa, “N bu alebu ŋon ta yem. Weera te n ne naase de Gbeŋgyoo ŋon diyem bela”
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, "I tell you the truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you. Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day."

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Keŋte Yesu baake ɔ kaseela baŋ kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ te ɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ nombia dana maŋ waraŋase, nawolo nyi gyɛŋ weeya kpaakpaa atooro yaa ŋgba be kyaa me gyaŋ te be te dii ta kolo ya nɛ. Mɔɔ ne gyae maa lɔ wɔ gbɛɛ na tanam ya, nawolo nyi be sia de kaa biri wɔ gbɛɛnaa man.”
translated: When Jesus came to them, he saw their faith and said to them, “Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has not risen anyone greater than John the Baptist; yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he. From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has suffered violence, and the violent take it by force. For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John. And if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who was to come.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: Mɔna ɛ gyu na ɛ kaa gyueŋ kpene keŋ Wurubuarɛ nombii aŋmaraseŋ see kei na wolo nɛ man. Be ŋmarasewɔ nyiaa, Wurubuarɛ kpa, ‘Na kedeesa te maŋ Wurubuarɛ ne gyae lee ɛ gyaŋ ya. Keŋ mɛɛ gyae yɛna nyi yaa wii dɔŋa waraŋase.’ Ma te kɔŋ nyi me kaa baake balaŋ baŋ be gbɛɛneŋ wulaa tenɛɛ Wurubuarɛ siaman nɛ ya, mɔna me kɔŋae me kaa baake nombiakumɛɛyɛɛra.” Be bɔɔse Yesu nɔɔkebake wose man nombia Maake
translated: Mona, he went and said to the people, “See, I tell you, I am not going to let you go until you have seen the Son of Man’s power. And I will give you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions, and nothing will harm you. But do not rejoice in this, that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”  He then said to his disciples, “I tell you, it is not right to rejoice in this, that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.” He said this to them, and then he sent them out to preach the gospel.

ref: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Walaŋ ŋon ɔ be duu abɔɔ bia kpaakpaa ŋan nɛ yɛna maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon,
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Woromɔ te de naa neŋ nyi n yɛɛ ŋɔɔle te de lɛɛ neŋ gyu da akpaŋalaŋse man, yaa n dɔŋɛɛ diteŋteli te de faa neŋ abɔɔ n doowɔ?
translated: Is it true that you have been given a new job? Tell me, what kind of work is it?

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Te ɔ yela balaŋ baŋ tisiwɔ kyakee tɛɛle man. Keŋte Yesu moo bodobodose anoŋ na kpebia bala baŋ, te ɔ gyinaa ɔ nyee adido te ɔ fanewɔ do Wurubuarɛ nɔɔ, te ɔ kɛrɛɛ ŋa te ɔ moo ŋa fa ɔ kpilala baŋ, te baŋ mɔ moowɔ kpɛlɛɛ balaŋ baŋ.
translated: And he said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Whoever humbles themselves like this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven. And whoever receives one such child in my name receives me, but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to sin, it would be better for him to have a great millstone fastened around his neck and to be drowned in the depth of the sea.

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Ao! Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ balaŋ baŋ ɛ bɛɛ lɛɛ maŋ di ya, te ɛ yɛɛ balaŋ kumɛɛwɔ. Weeya aŋmoŋ te kaboena nyi maa kyaa ɛ gyaŋ, na maa nyiŋ konɔɔ fa ŋon?” Keŋte ɔ yako balee ŋon nyiaa, “Mo n bu ŋon kɔna maŋ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, because you shut the kingdom of heaven against people. For you neither enter yourselves nor allow those who are entering to go in.  Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you travel across sea and land to make one convert, and when he is converted, you make him twice as much a son of hell as yourselves.

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Keŋte Yesu leewɔ be gyaŋ donɔɔ keŋ man, te ɔ gyuuwɔ Bɛtani donɔɔ man, te ɔ doowɔ botɔɔ. Yesu ke falaa fiigi daŋ keŋ Maake
translated: Jesus went out to Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John had been baptizing.  Jesus came from Galilee.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi mɔɔ gyae maa tekaa nyɔɔ soloŋ kei bela ya, kelii wee keŋ daa na ŋon dɛɛ nyɔɔ soloŋ wɔle me kya Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man.”
translated: If you want to eat, eat alone, don't let anyone see you, because if they see you, they will say that you are a thief and you will be punished by the Wurubuarɛ people.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Mena dɔɔ ɛ na yeyɛɛ na kyoo wui nombia ya, te ɛ na mo gyɛŋ wui nombia kpu kyoo wui man ya. Nawolo nyi wee kamasɛ na ke wui nombia.”
translated: If you are a man, you should be strong and brave. If you are a woman, you should be strong and brave. You should all be strong and brave.

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Keŋ dɔɔ te Yesu be yako nombia kɛŋa yɛna nyiaa, na balaŋ ne yako nyi feliŋkum doo la ɔ man. Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia Mateo
translated: When Jesus came to his own, his own people did not receive him. But to those who did receive him, he gave the right to become children of God.
